scenario_results.reason: Produced in-scope routing with dependency-aware handoff output and complete ownership/condition context.
Returned explicit prerequisites and blocker set when telemetry, tenant continuity policy, and facility evidence were incomplete.
Blocked handoff due to missing prior owner, destination owner, and artifacts, preserving required context-first behavior.
Raised shared-ownership ambiguity with required arbitration conditions before continuation.
Refused execution ownership and returned adjacent specialist routing with evidence context.
Escalated maintenance/dependency collisions and blocked final closure until sequencing was reconciled.
Mapped data-center and cloud-specific terminology to normalized output artifacts before routing.
Detected public-source and tenant policy conflict and escalated without inferring resolution.
Returned boundary-safe adjacent-lane routing for legal/security-notification authorities.
Blocked closure and required destination proof and signoff evidence before completion.
Captured tenant-specific adaptation and escalated outcome until policy confirmation was provided.
Returned low-confidence escalation with missing authority matrix and blocked handoff as required.

notes: All 12 scenarios referenced by manifest/evaluation/scenarios.md are represented in scenario_coverage.
No missing ability IDs remain in required map coverage.
Runtime remains specification-based and does not claim retained-implementation authority.
Commercialization and deployment constraints are documented separately and cross-linked.
